rationale: The cited paper directly studies how price and advertising elasticities (i.e., marketing effectiveness) vary with external conditions, which closely aligns with the citing paper’s core claim that soda taxes change the effectiveness of price/promotion tools. Even though the contexts and methods differ (U.K. business-cycle ECM/3SLS vs. U.S. soda-tax DID), this looks plausibly like a substantive conceptual prior rather than an incidental mention. With this construct-level overlap, it should be retained for full relation judgment.
